claim: For 4096 rows by 1000 fp32 columns on AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT gfx1201, the block-per-row shared-memory softmax measured 4.763268x faster than the one-thread-per-row baseline using HIP-event timing only.
expected_profiler_signals: stable tail handling for non-power-of-two columns
curation_note: timing-only non-power-of-two column sweep: HIP-event timings and correctness passed, but rocprofiler/rocprof counters were not collected because rocprof, rocprofv3, rocminfo, and rocm-smi were unavailable in this Windows venv environment. The optimized run includes one implausibly tiny min sample, so use median and p10/p90 rather than best-of timing.
